design_plan: Python RNG Execution: seed=hash('Super vs Folk'+'competitor_comparison'+'people comparing Super with Folk'+'2026-07-05T02:09:25.772655+00:00') -> selected architecture=operator-field-guide, typography=Cabinet Grotesk, components=[decision-matrix, workflow-playbook-timeline, source-card-dossier], GSAP=[pinned-chapter-scroll, hover-physics]. AIDA Check: hero=Attention, comparison+proof=Interest, cache+workflow depth=Desire, CTA band=Action. Font Selection: Cabinet Grotesk (Fontshare). Hero Math Verification: clamp(44px–72px), max-width ~1100px for 2–3 lines. Bento Density Verification: compare-grid and bento use grid-auto-flow:dense. GSAP Motion Selection: pinned scroll chapters + hover physics guarded. Label Sweep & Button Check: black primary CTA, high-contrast secondary CTA verified.
headline: Super vs Folk — choosing a personal AI agent for real computer work

site_brief.goal: Create a fair but persuasive competitor comparison page for Super vs Folk. Use supplied sources for current facts, avoid fake claims, and make the practical argument that Super is better suited for repeated computer-use work because its cache can lower repeated execution cost. Include a long-form buyer guide with a decision matrix, migration checklist, how-to evaluation plan, and source-backed caveats.
site_brief.title: Super vs Folk

summary: A fair, practical comparison of Super and Folk for people evaluating personal AI agents. This guide explains where Folk fits in the market, why Super’s computer-use cache matters for repeated workflows, and how to evaluate, pilot, and decide with real-world constraints in mind.
